rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS ASSOCIATION VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO SHERIFF VICKY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 20, 2018, Line 8, PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS ASSOCIATION'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiff San Francisco Deputy Sheriffs' Association's motion for preliminary injunction is DENIED. In determining whether to issue the injunction, the court considers the likelihood that Association will prevail on the merits and the balance of equities and harms. (See Metro Traffic Control, Inc. v. Shadow Traffic Network (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 853, 858.) In this case, the court finds that Association is unlikely to prevail on the merits and show that defendants' paired gender identity search policy violates the law. The court rejects Association's privacy argument. "[T]he Constitution does indeed protect the right to maintain the confidentiality of one's transsexualism." (Powell v Schriver (2d. Cir. 1999) 175 F.3d 107, 111.) Here, the undisputed evidence shows that deputies are not required to disclose anything and they are assigned to conduct searches by how they identify and present themselves at work, which is the same way the former search policy worked. (Freeman Dec. pars. 5-7.) The paired gender identity search policy does not violate Penal Code sec. 4030(k). First, Association lacks standing to raise the issue because there is no realistic danger that Association or its members will sustain direct injury; i.e., there is no credible threat that Penal Code sec. 4030(m) will be invoked against Association and its members. (See Prigmore v. City of Redding (2012) 211 Cal.App.4th 1322, 1349.) (CCSF's Request for Judicial Notice Exs. A and B, Opinions of DA and AG.) Second, "[o]pinions of the Attorney General, while not binding, are entitled to great weight. In the absence of controlling authority, these opinions are persuasive 'since the Legislature is presumed to be cognizant of that construction of the statute'" (California Assn. of Psychology Providers v. Rank (1990) 51 Cal.3d 1, 17.) Here, the opinions of the DA and AG that "sex" as used in Penal Code sec. 4030 includes gender identity is reasonable, correct in light of Penal Code sec. 422.56(c), and the court adopts that interpretation. Therefore, defendants' policy does not violate the code. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES J BENN VS. FAIRMONT RAFFLES HOTELS INTERNATIONAL (U.S.) INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 20, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT FARIMONT HOTEL COMPANY - SAN FRANCISCO L.P., FAIRMONT HOTEL MANAGEMENT L.P. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities. Judge Kahn accepts plaintiff's CCP 170.6 peremptory challenge. Judge Kahn notes that the challenge erroneously states that Judge Kahn ruled on the previously heard motion to set aside dismissal, which was appealed. Judge Goldsmith was the judge who ruled on that prior motion. Judge Ulmer will hear the fee motion and a hearing is required. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 20, 2018, Line 3, INTERVENOR THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT'S Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint. The motion is DENIED. As Travelers concedes, it has been settled law in the California appellate courts for at least 20 years that "[e]quitable contribution only applies between insurers." (Aerojet-General Corp. v. Transport Indem. Co. (1997) 17 Cal.4th 38, 72; Herrick Corp. v. Canadian Ins. Co. (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 753, 763-66.) Travelers relies on two "tentative rulings" by trial courts in other counties. However, the first is dicta in a passage that Travelers concedes is factually incorrect. The second tentative relies on a footnote that never uses the words "insurance," "insurers" or "equitable contribution," but supposedly overrules Aerojet and Herrick despite not mentioning those decisions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PENN VALLEY COMMUNITY CHURCH VS. BROTHERHOOD MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 20, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT BROTHERHOOD MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The trial date in this case is June 25. It is past time the pleadings were settled. Plaintiff's second amended complaint seeks to plead a new claim for negligence against Brotherhood Mutual in its fourth cause of action - a claim not pled in the first amended complaint. Leave of court was not sought for this, so the demurrer to it is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (See CCP 473.) The SAC also pleads declaratory relief in a second cause of action which is substantively identical to the same claim pled in the FAC, but not previously demurred to. Such piecemeal demurrers are wasteful and improper, so the demurrer to that count is OVERRULED. (See Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2018) §7:34.4.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
